summary: MindEcho backend + frontend fully tested against the public preview URL. Backend: all 17 pytest cases passed (auth register/login/me, text analyze with Claude 4.6 stress vs calm probability sanity, history list/detail/delete, stats shape, MongoDB _id never leaks). Frontend (390x844 mobile viewport): login screen renders with correct testIDs, fresh-user registration navigates to /(tabs)/dashboard with 'Hello, <name>' greeting, latest reflection card / weekly bars / quick-action cards render, text tab analyzes a stressful sentence with real Claude (~15s) and navigates to /result/[id] which shows STRESS LEVEL 'High' 97%, key-features list, highlighted words ('terrified', 'panicking') visible with colored background, 'A gentle nudge' recommendation and Download PDF button. History tab lists both entries, tapping an item opens result. Profile screen reachable via the avatar and Sign Out returns user to /auth/login. Voice/multimodal endpoints were intentionally NOT tested (mic input can't be exercised via Playwright, as noted in the request). No deprecated Expo packages detected.

minor.issue: Missing token returns 403 (from FastAPI's HTTPBearer default) instead of 401. Frontend still works since it treats any non-200 as unauthenticated, but returning 401 is more RFC-correct. Consider HTTPBearer(auto_error=False) + manual 401.

react_native_issues.issue: React-web console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — coming from a nested view in LinearGradient. Cosmetic only, does not break UI, but worth updating to style.pointerEvents to silence the warning. Reference: /app/frontend/app/auth/login.tsx:36, /app/frontend/app/auth/register.tsx:38, /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/dashboard.tsx:41

action_items: (Optional) Return 401 instead of 403 when Authorization header is missing on protected routes (use HTTPBearer(auto_error=False)).
(Optional) Replace `props.pointerEvents` with `style={{ pointerEvents: ... }}` wherever it is passed as a prop to silence the RN-web deprecation warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 468-line file — still under the 700-line threshold, but consider splitting into routers/services (auth, analysis, history) as the project grows.
All MongoDB queries correctly exclude _id via projections and by popping it before returning — verified via grep on multiple JSON responses.
All backend routes use the required /api prefix and are reachable through the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL.
Frontend uses process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL, StyleSheet.create everywhere, all text wrapped in <Text>, all interactive elements have testIDs.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (auth, text-analyze, history, profile, signout) — voice recording intentionally skipped

seed_data_creation: Registered one fresh QA user (qa+<ts>@mindecho.dev / password123) and created 2 text analyses (1 stressful, 1 calm) via the test suite; the calm one was deleted by the delete-history test. Also exercised the pre-seeded test@mindecho.dev account.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public backend URL is https://emotion-detect-ai-4.preview.emergentagent.com (with /api prefix). Seed user test@mindecho.dev / password123 already exists. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_mindecho_api.py checks for `"_id"` (with quotes) rather than the raw substring `_id` because `user_id` contains that substring. Voice endpoints (/api/analyze/voice, /api/analyze/multimodal) were NOT exercised — they need real mic audio and would consume Whisper quota.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in the flows requested.
